key_insight: This pipeline automatically detects moles in wide-angle skin photos, segments lesion boundaries, and converts each lesion into ABCD(E)-style quantitative scores for screening support.
clinical_relevance: It could help standardize melanoma triage and longitudinal mole tracking, especially in settings with limited specialist access or when using smartphone images.
method: Adaptive thresholding and blob analysis to detect candidate moles
EDSR super-resolution plus brightness-based filtering to reduce false positives
BAM segmentation using EfficientNet-B7 activations and GradCAM, followed by K-means color scoring
evidence_signal: In the abstract, the system reports 86% F1 for mole detection on 87 wide-angle images, 90.45% accuracy on ISIC2017 for BAM, and melanoma AUC of 0.99 for the EfficientNet-B7 classifier.
watch_item: The reported performance is promising, but the pipeline still needs external clinical validation and dermatologist concordance testing before real-world use.
paper_title: Automated Melanoma Screening: A Machine Learning Pipeline for Mole Detection, Boundary Segmentation, and ABCD(E) Feature Extraction
